base_case: Biakanja v. Irving — SYNTHETIC COUNTERFACTUAL VARIANT (not a real decided case)
constructor: Venkat Porto (drafted by Claude Opus stand-in; auto-generated from base instance)

perturbations.original_text: Whether the transaction was specifically intended to affect the plaintiff is unresolved on this record. The bequest was contingent on multiple conditions and the recipient's status as intended beneficiary was diffuse. The court does not develop intent-to-affect as a load-bearing consideration.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (moral blame) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f1 (moral_blame). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The foreseeability of harm to the recipient was remote because the bequest was conditional on many factors (other named relatives' deaths, testator's longevity) and the recipient's receipt was uncertain regardless of whether the will was validly executed.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (probability of harm) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f2 (probability_of_harm). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The certainty of the plaintiff's injury was low because the conditional-bequest structure made receipt uncertain even if the will had been executed correctly. Damages are speculative.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (certainty of injury) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f3 (certainty_of_injury). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The causal connection between the document preparer's conduct and any alleged harm to the recipient was heavily attenuated by multiple intervening events — subsequent deaths of named relatives, changes in testator circumstances, and the conditional structure of the bequest itself.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (scope of risk) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f4 (scope_of_risk). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The document preparer was a licensed attorney in good standing acting within the scope of their profession — not an unauthorized practitioner. Moral blame for the preparation is minimal.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (moral blame) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f5 (moral_blame). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: Imposing a duty here would not meaningfully deter future harm because the conduct at issue is already fully regulated by attorney licensing and malpractice law. The duty extension would burden legitimate estate-planning practice without adding preventive value.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (public policy externality) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
